assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable/payable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange exposure within the group.

The question asks for the IFRS-supported hedge-documentation routes relevant to this exposure in consolidated financial statements, including alternatives that may apply at different stages of the transaction.

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the FX effects are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for ordinary intragroup items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 sets the default boundary that intragroup items are not eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements unless a specific foreign-currency exception applies.

approaches.label: Forecast intragroup transaction hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately allows the foreign currency risk of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction to be hedged in consolidation if it is in another currency and will affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 provide a distinct consolidated FX hedge model for a net investment in a foreign operation, with the effective portion recognised in OCI and reclassified on disposal.
